description: Add a client portal SSO discovery endpoint at POST /api/auth/client-portal/sso/discover.
Add a client portal SSO resolution endpoint at POST /api/auth/client-portal/sso/resolve.
Resolve client portal tenant context from a valid tenant slug.
Resolve client portal tenant context from an active portal domain.
Resolve client portal tenant context from a trusted client portal callback URL when applicable.
Return a neutral no-provider discovery response when tenant context cannot be resolved.
Return tenant-configured SSO providers from client portal discovery when tenant context is resolved.
Rate-limit client portal SSO discovery using anti-enumeration-safe keys and responses.
Issue signed client portal SSO discovery state with an explicit client-portal audience.
Validate client portal SSO resolution against signed discovery state, provider, tenant, and callback URL.
Issue signed client portal SSO resolution state that cannot be reused by MSP SSO.
Make SsoProviderButtons configurable by auth surface, discovery endpoint, resolution endpoint, and storage key.
Wire ClientLoginForm to render client portal SSO buttons only when tenant context is available.
Pass client portal email, callback URL, tenant slug, and portal domain context into SSO button discovery and resolution.
Start OAuth with client portal login state including mode=login, user_type=client, and tenant hint.
Require client portal OAuth profile mapping to use user_type=client.
Require client portal OAuth profile mapping to match the resolved tenant.
Prevent client portal OAuth mapping from falling back to global client-user lookup.
Ensure internal users cannot authenticate through the client portal OAuth flow.
Apply existing vanity-domain handoff redirect logic to successful OAuth client portal sign-ins.
Finalize and clear client portal SSO state after OAuth sign-in succeeds or fails.
Preserve existing client portal credentials login behavior.
Preserve existing MSP SSO discovery and resolution behavior.
Expose autoLinkClient in the SSO settings UI separately from internal auto-linking.
Add MSP workspace default client portal Entra provisioning mode configuration defaulting to disabled, with built-in and workflow-managed modes.
Add tenant setting deactivateEntraManagedPortalUsersOnEntitlementRemoval defaulting to true.
Add settings UI controls for MSP workspace default client portal Entra provisioning mode and entitlement-removal deactivation.
Persist per-client mapping override for client portal provisioning mode with inherit-workspace-default, disabled, built-in, and workflow-managed choices.
Persist selected Entra group ID as the client portal access entitlement source for a mapped client.
Persist group membership mode for entitlement checks with transitive membership as the default.
Load selectable Entra groups for a managed tenant/client entitlement configuration UI.
Validate that selected entitlement groups belong to the configured managed Entra tenant.
Extend Entra sync user data or sync context with portal access group membership information.
Check transitive group membership for client portal entitlement during Entra sync.
Run client portal provisioning only after contact reconciliation succeeds without ambiguity.
Skip portal user provisioning and workflow events when the effective client portal Entra provisioning mode resolves to disabled.
Skip portal user provisioning when no entitlement group is configured for the mapped client.
Skip portal user provisioning when the Entra user lacks email and UPN.
Skip or deactivate portal access when the Entra account is disabled.
Skip portal user provisioning when the Entra user is not in the configured access group.
Find an existing client portal user for a reconciled contact before creating a new one.
Find an existing client portal user by tenant and email only when it is safe and non-conflicting.
Create a client portal user for an entitled reconciled contact when no portal user exists.
Set created portal users to user_type=client with tenant, contact_id, client ID, and normalized email.
Mark provisioned portal users as email-verified or otherwise OAuth-ready without requiring password setup.
Upsert Microsoft OAuth account links for Entra-provisioned portal users using stable Entra identity data.
Store durable metadata identifying portal users as Entra-managed.
Store source Entra tenant ID, managed tenant mapping, Entra object ID, and entitlement source metadata for Entra-managed portal users.
Detect conflicting portal users by tenant, contact, email, and link state before provisioning.
Skip provisioning and record a skipped/conflict result when conflicting portal users are found.
Deactivate Entra-managed portal users when group entitlement is removed and the deactivation setting is enabled.
Deactivate Entra-managed portal users when their Entra account is disabled.
Do not deactivate manually managed portal users when Entra entitlement is missing.
Track whether a portal user was deactivated by Entra lifecycle versus manually disabled.
Reactivate Entra-managed portal users only when entitlement returns and prior deactivation was lifecycle-owned.
Return or persist sync provisioning counts for created, linked, deactivated, skipped, and ambiguous outcomes.
Show client portal provisioning counts in the existing Entra sync results UI where available.
Extend portal invitation flow to optionally pre-link an Entra identity for an invited contact.
Support invitation completion through OAuth without forcing password setup when an Entra pre-link exists.
Keep invitation-created manual portal users outside Entra deactivation unless explicitly marked Entra-managed.
Update client portal SSO tests that previously asserted SSO was disabled to assert tenant-gated behavior.
Update OpenAPI route inventory or auth API docs for new client portal SSO endpoints if the repo generation path requires it.
Persist workspace default role and optional per-client default role override for built-in Entra-provisioned users, defaulting to User.
Assign the configured default Alga client portal role only when creating a new portal user in built-in provisioning mode.
Preserve manual Alga role changes on existing Entra-managed portal users during later Entra sync runs.
Show warning copy when an MSP selects a broad Entra entitlement group, explaining that every enabled user in the group is eligible for portal access.
Ensure built-in provisioning and workflow-managed provisioning are mutually exclusive for each mapped client sync decision.
Persist workflow target/configuration for workflow-managed client portal Entra provisioning mode.
Publish an idempotent workflow event when an entitled Entra user becomes eligible for client portal access in workflow-managed mode.
Publish an idempotent workflow event when Entra group entitlement is removed or the Entra account is disabled in workflow-managed mode.
Include tenant, client, contact, Entra identity, entitlement, account status, configured group, recommended default role, and sync run context in workflow-managed provisioning events.
Add or expose a workflow action that safely creates or links a client portal user using the same idempotent primitives as built-in provisioning.
Add or expose workflow actions for assigning a client portal role and upserting the Microsoft OAuth account link.
Add or expose workflow actions for deactivating and reactivating Entra-managed client portal access without changing manually managed users.
Show workflow-managed mode copy explaining that the selected workflow is responsible for provisioning, role assignment, invitations, and lifecycle changes.
Resolve effective client portal provisioning mode by using the per-client mapping override when set, otherwise the MSP workspace default.
Keep portal access group selection explicit per client mapping; do not inherit a group globally across managed Microsoft tenants.
Update configuration UI terminology to distinguish MSP workspace, Alga client, managed Microsoft tenant, and client mapping.
Thread workspace default provisioning mode and default role into Entra sync activity context so sync can compute effective per-client behavior.
Treat workflow-managed mode as the effective mode only after default/override resolution, so built-in mutations and workflow events remain mutually exclusive.
